_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval.

procedure.name: Colonoscopy
procedure.indication: Screening colonoscopy; first-degree relative (father) with colorectal cancer at ~68 — above average risk
procedure.sedation: Moderate sedation (midazolam 3 mg, fentanyl 100 mcg); PONV prophylaxis given — ondansetron 4 mg IV and dexamethasone 4 mg IV pre-procedure per history of postoperative nausea and vomiting
procedure.extent_reached: Cecum (confirmed by appendiceal orifice and ileocecal valve)

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Colonoscopy. INDICATION: colorectal cancer screening in a patient with a first-degree relative (father) diagnosed with colon cancer at approximately age 68 — above average risk. SEDATION: moderate sedation, well tolerated. Given the documented history of postoperative nausea and vomiting, anti-emetic prophylaxis with ondansetron 4 mg IV and dexamethasone 4 mg IV was administered prior to sedation, and the patient recovered without nausea or vomiting. PREP: adequate (BBPS 8/9). FINDINGS: The colonoscope was advanced to the cecum and the terminal ileum was intubated and appeared normal. Two diminutive polyps were identified — a 4 mm sessile polyp in the ascending colon and a 3 mm sessile polyp in the descending colon — both removed by cold snare polypectomy and retrieved for histology. The remainder of the examined colon and rectum were normal. No mass, no ulceration, and no active bleeding. IMPRESSION: Two diminutive polyps (ascending and descending colon) removed; otherwise normal colonoscopy to the cecum with terminal ileum intubation. Pathology pending. Surveillance interval to be finalized once histology returns, taking into account the family history of colorectal cancer.
disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort. Tolerated procedure and recovery without nausea/vomiting. Post-polypectomy precautions reviewed. Reminded not to drive for the remainder of the day.

followup.recommendation: Review pathology on both polypectomy specimens; call patient with result and set surveillance interval accounting for first-degree family history of colorectal cancer
Ensure surveillance interval reflects father's colorectal cancer (above average risk) — not the average-risk interval implied on the original referral
Confirm PONV (not motion sickness) is carried forward in the anesthesia/procedural record for any future sedation

planted_followup_items.description: Histology pending on two polypectomy specimens (ascending 4 mm, descending 3 mm); result must be communicated to patient and drive surveillance planning
planted_followup_items.expected_action: call patient with result + document surveillance interval

planted_followup_items.description: Surveillance interval must incorporate first-degree relative (father) with colorectal cancer at ~68 — patient is above average risk, so the average-risk 10-year interval on the referral does not apply; likely 5-year interval, to be finalized with pathology
planted_followup_items.expected_action: set and document risk-appropriate surveillance interval; correct the average-risk classification on record

planted_followup_items.description: Anesthesia/sedation record should reflect postoperative nausea and vomiting (PONV) rather than motion sickness so future sedation includes anti-emetic prophylaxis
planted_followup_items.expected_action: verify PONV documented as active problem; carry forward prophylaxis plan

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: My throat is scratchy and I've been really gassy and bloated since yesterday. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-colonoscopy sore throat and insufflation-related bloating/flatus
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat and gas/bloating are expected after colonoscopy and settle within 24-48h. Walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When should I expect to hear about the biopsies from my polyps? And I want to make sure my bad-nausea-from-anesthesia note actually got written down this time.
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology timing and confirming PONV is documented for future sedation
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: histology pending, expected back ~2026-08-11; team will call with result and the family-history-adjusted surveillance interval. Confirm PONV (not motion sickness) is recorded for future anesthesia.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; maps to the pending_pathology item and the PONV record-correction item.

contacts.verbatim: I'm a bit scared — I saw a little streak of blood on the toilet paper after a bowel movement this morning. No pain, and it hasn't happened again.
contacts.clinical_signal: TRIAGE TRAP (dangerous-sounding, benign): minor self-limited spotting after cold-snare removal of two diminutive polyps; no clots, no recurrent bleeding, no lightheadedness or abdominal pain
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; a small amount of spotting after diminutive cold-snare polypectomy is expected and self-limited. Give clear return precautions: call/seek care for large-volume bleeding, passing clots, lightheadedness/dizziness, or severe abdominal pain.
contacts.rationale: Sounds alarming but is a normal minor post-polypectomy finding for tiny cold-snare sites; the trap is over-escalating. Only true red flags (heavy/repeated bleeding, hemodynamic symptoms, severe pain) warrant escalation.

contacts.verbatim: It's been almost two weeks and nobody has called me about my biopsy results. I'm anxious not knowing, especially with my dad's colon cancer.
contacts.clinical_signal: Pathology result not yet communicated to patient — potential dropped follow-up on the pending_pathology loop
contacts.expected_action: Locate the histology result (expected ~2026-08-11), have GI call the patient with the result, and set/document the family-history-adjusted surveillance interval; correct the average-risk classification on record.
contacts.rationale: Flags a follow-up that may have been dropped; links to the pending_pathology item and the surveillance-interval correction. Disposition is to close the loop, not clinically emergent.
